correction.rationale: Use positive points distributed across the visible ankle-foot, midfoot, ball, and toes, while suppressing the upper lower-leg/background region, gray floor, and the separate rear-foot area. Refine the ankle boundary and remove rectangular artifacts and holes.

judgments.evidence: Fail: the instance identity is consistent with right_foot_base, but the proposed mask is contaminated by background/adjacent ankle area and contains severe blocky artifacts and holes. A point-based SAM refinement is safer than a hand-traced polygon because the toe separations and ankle boundary are detailed.

observations.contour: The contour is jagged and discontinuous, with large artificial steps around the ankle and top of the foot. The toe edge is closer to correct but still inherits noisy boundaries.
observations.full_context: The candidate is the character's right foot according to the semantic pose-chain evidence, despite its viewer-side placement. The visible foot is planted in front of the other leg and has a clear ankle-to-toes silhouette.
observations.mask: The mask has numerous rectangular block artifacts and internal holes, extends into background near the upper/right ankle region, and does not provide a clean anatomically plausible foot contour.
observations.neighbor_overlap: There is likely overlap with the adjacent lower leg/ankle and background; the mask should stop at the visible foot-base/ankle boundary and exclude the separate rear foot and surrounding gray floor.
observations.overlay: Red coverage broadly follows the foot but spills into the gray background and upper ankle/lower-leg area. Several sharp horizontal and vertical edges are unrelated to the visible anatomy.
observations.source_crop: The foot and toes are clearly visible, but the ankle transition is complex and the crop also contains the lower leg and background around the foot.
